System templates (welcome email, password reset, billing notifications) cannot be deleted but can be customized in appearance. Add your organization logo and adjust the color scheme from Settings > Brand > Email Branding.

Unsubscribe links are required for marketing emails. For transactional emails (notifications, alerts), they are optional but recommended.

docs.title: Localization and Timezones
docs.content:
## Localization and Timezones

Timestamps are stored in UTC and displayed in each user's local timezone. Set your timezone in Account Settings > Preferences > Timezone. If not set, the browser's detected timezone is used.

Date fields without a time component display without timezone conversion. A due date of Jan 15 is Jan 15 for all users regardless of timezone. Datetime fields include time and are displayed in the viewer's local timezone.

When filtering by date ranges via the API, all datetimes must be in ISO 8601 format with UTC offset.

The interface language is set per user in Account Settings > Preferences > Language. Exported CSV and JSON files always use period as decimal separator and ISO 8601 for dates, regardless of locale.

docs.title: Backup and Restore
docs.content:
## Backup and Restore

We perform automated daily backups of all data with a 30-day retention period stored in a geographically separate region. Backups are encrypted and are not directly accessible to customers. They are used by our team for disaster recovery.

For your own backup copies, use the scheduled export feature to deliver exports to your S3 bucket on a daily or weekly basis.

Point-in-time restore: if you accidentally delete records, soft-deleted records are recoverable for 30 days from Settings > Data > Trash. Permanent deletions cannot be recovered through self-service. Contact support within 24 hours for a potential restore from backup.

For major incidents, contact support immediately. We can restore to a point in time within the last 30 days. Restore operations take 2-8 hours depending on data volume.

docs.title: Multi-Workspace Management
docs.content:
## Multi-Workspace Management

An organization can have multiple workspaces, one per department or client for example. Each workspace has independent data, settings, and member lists. Users can belong to multiple workspaces under the same organization.

Create a new workspace from the workspace switcher in the top left navigation. Only organization owners can create workspaces.

Users are invited to workspaces individually. Being a member of one workspace does not grant access to others. An organization admin can view and manage all workspaces from the Admin Console.

Billing is aggregated at the organization level across all workspaces. A single invoice covers all workspace seat counts combined.

Cross-workspace data sharing is not supported. Records cannot be linked between workspaces. Copying records between workspaces requires exporting from one and importing into the other.

docs.title: SSO with Azure AD
docs.content:
## SSO with Azure AD

Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) SAML integration is supported and tested on Business and Enterprise plans.

In the Azure portal, create a new Enterprise Application. Under Single sign-on, choose SAML. Fill in the Identifier (Entity ID) and Reply URL (ACS URL) from your SSO settings page.

Attribute mapping for Azure AD:
- User.userprincipalname maps to email (NameID)
- User.givenname maps to firstName
- User.surname maps to lastName

Azure AD by default sends the UPN as the NameID. If users log in with a different email than their UPN, change the NameID claim to use user.mail instead.

Group-based access: configure Azure AD to only allow specific groups to use the application. Users not in the assigned groups cannot log in via SSO even if they have an Azure AD account.

docs.title: SSO with Okta
docs.content:
## SSO with Okta

Okta SAML integration is fully supported and is the most commonly used IdP in our customer base. Setup typically takes under 30 minutes.

In Okta, go to Applications > Create App Integration > SAML 2.0. In the SAML settings, enter the Single Sign On URL (your ACS URL) and the Audience URI (your Entity ID), both found in Settings > Security > SSO.

Attribute statements in Okta:
- user.email maps to email
- user.firstName maps to firstName
- user.lastName maps to lastName

JIT provisioning: when a new user logs in via Okta SSO for the first time, an account is created automatically. If the email matches an existing account, the SSO identity is linked to it.

Okta's User Import feature combined with SCIM provides fully automated provisioning and deprovisioning.

docs.title: Compliance and Data Residency
docs.content:
## Compliance and Data Residency

By default, all customer data is stored in the US (AWS us-east-1). EU data residency is available on Enterprise plans, storing all data in AWS eu-west-1 (Ireland).

To request EU data residency, contact your account manager before your first data is written. Migrating an existing workspace to a different region requires a planned maintenance window.

Compliance certifications held: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and GDPR-ready (DPA available). HIPAA BAA is available for Enterprise customers with a signed agreement.

Data processing addendum (DPA): available for EU and UK organizations as required by GDPR. Sign via Settings > Legal > Data Processing Agreement. The DPA covers standard contractual clauses for international data transfers.

We publish a Trust Center with current certifications, sub-processor list, security policies, and penetration test summaries.

docs.title: Custom Domain Setup
docs.content:
## Custom Domain Setup

Enterprise plans can host the workspace on a custom domain instead of the default subdomain.

To set up a custom domain: go to Settings > Workspace > Custom Domain, enter the desired hostname, and add the provided CNAME record to your DNS configuration. DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours but usually completes within one hour.

SSL certificate provisioning is automatic. The certificate is provisioned once the CNAME is live and renews automatically every 90 days.

Email notifications, SSO callbacks, and API documentation links use the custom domain once it is active. Existing bookmarks to the default subdomain redirect automatically via a 301 redirect.

If you need to remove or change the custom domain, update the IdP ACS URL before making the DNS change to avoid SSO breakage.

docs.title: Webhook Rate Limits
docs.content:
## Webhook Rate Limits

Webhook delivery rate limits exist on both the sending side (events delivered per second to your endpoint) and the receiving side (how fast your endpoint must respond).

Delivery rate: we deliver up to 200 events per second per webhook endpoint. Events exceeding this are queued. The queue holds up to 10,000 events. If the queue fills, newer events are dropped and logged.

Response timeout: your endpoint must respond within 10 seconds. Responses that take longer are treated as failures and trigger the retry sequence. If your handler takes more than a second, respond with HTTP 200 immediately and process the payload asynchronously.

Best practice: use a message queue (SQS, RabbitMQ) as your webhook endpoint. The endpoint immediately returns 200 and enqueues the payload for a worker to process.

Store the event ID from each payload and check for duplicates before processing to ensure idempotency.

docs.title: Embedding Reports
docs.content:
## Embedding Reports

Reports can be embedded in external websites, internal wikis, or customer-facing portals using an iframe embed code. Access the embed code from any report via Share > Embed.

The embed URL includes a signed token that grants read-only access without requiring the viewer to log in. Tokens expire after 24 hours and are automatically refreshed when the page loads.

Configure what the embedded viewer can interact with: date range filter (allow or fix), dimension drill-down, and CSV export. More permissive settings improve the viewer experience but expose more data.

Embed tokens are scoped to the specific report or dashboard and cannot access other workspace data.

For customer-facing embeds where different customers should see different data subsets, use the API to generate report URLs with pre-applied filters combined with signed short-lived tokens.

docs.title: Offline Mode
docs.content:
## Offline Mode

The web application supports limited offline functionality. When connectivity is lost, a banner appears. You can continue viewing recently accessed records and adding comments. Changes are queued locally.

When connection is restored, queued changes sync automatically in the order they were made. Conflicts are resolved by the last-write-wins rule with a notification showing what changed.

Offline mode is not available for: creating new records, loading records not previously visited in the session, bulk operations, and file uploads.

The mobile apps have more robust offline support. Records are cached based on recency and starred status. The mobile app can create and edit records offline.

Offline mode is not suitable for mission-critical real-time operations. Build API integrations with proper retry logic rather than relying on the browser UI's offline queue.

docs.title: User Deactivation vs Deletion
docs.content:
## User Deactivation vs Deletion

When a team member leaves your organization, you have two options: deactivate or delete.

Deactivation (recommended): the user can no longer log in and does not consume a seat. All records, comments, and activity remain intact and attributed to them. Their account can be reactivated if they rejoin.

Deletion: removes the user account permanently. Their records and comments remain but become unattributed. Activity log entries retain the user ID for audit purposes but the display name is anonymized.

For GDPR right-to-erasure requests: deactivation alone does not satisfy an erasure request. Use the personal data anonymization endpoint to remove identifying information, then deactivate the account.

Admin users cannot be deactivated if they are the last admin. Transfer admin to another user first.

docs.title: Troubleshooting Login Issues
docs.content:
## Troubleshooting Login Issues

If you cannot log in, work through these steps before contacting support.

1. Check Caps Lock. Passwords are case-sensitive.
2. Try the password reset flow even if you think you know your password.
3. Check if your account is locked due to too many failed attempts. Wait 30 minutes or contact support to clear the lock.
4. If using SSO, verify the IdP is reachable and not experiencing an outage.
5. Try a different browser or a private window. Browser extensions and corrupted cache can interfere with login.
6. Check if your IP is allowlisted if your organization uses IP restrictions.
7. If you recently changed your email, log in with the new email address.
8. Check your organization's SSO policy. If SSO is enforced, you cannot log in with a password even if you have one set.

If none of these steps resolve the issue, contact support with your email, what you see on the login page, and your browser and OS.

docs.title: Command Palette
docs.content:
## Command Palette

The command palette (Cmd/Ctrl + K) provides keyboard-first access to navigation, search, and actions across the workspace. It is the fastest way to get anywhere without using the mouse.

Type to search across records, workspace pages, settings, users, and available actions. Results are ranked by recency and relevance. Use arrow keys to navigate and Enter to select.

Common uses:
- Jump to a record: type part of the record title
- Navigate to a settings page: type the setting name such as billing, integrations, or team
- Run a global action: type create, export, or invite to surface quick-action commands
- Switch workspace: type the workspace name to jump directly

The command palette learns from your usage and surfaces frequently used items higher in results over time.

docs.title: Changelog and Release Notes
docs.content:
## Changelog and Release Notes

We publish release notes for every product update. Subscribe to the changelog RSS feed or email newsletter to receive updates automatically.

Updates are categorized: New (net-new features), Improved (enhancements to existing features), and Fixed (bug fixes). Breaking changes are marked explicitly and communicated at least 30 days in advance via email to all organization admins.

The in-app What's New banner highlights recent changes relevant to your plan and role. Clicking an item links to the full changelog entry.

Major releases include a migration guide if any action is required from admins. Check for required migration actions before major releases.

We follow a continuous deployment model. Updates are released frequently rather than in large batched releases. Downtime is not required for most updates.

docs.title: Exporting Reports to PDF and CSV
docs.content:
## Exporting Reports to PDF and CSV

Any report or dashboard can be exported as PDF or CSV. Access the export option from the report's action menu.

PDF export captures the visual report as rendered. The PDF includes the report title, filter state at the time of export, and a timestamp. Reports wider than one page are split across multiple pages.

CSV export outputs the underlying data table. If the report has grouping or aggregations, the CSV reflects those. To export raw records, use the Data Export feature from Settings > Data > Export instead.

Scheduled PDF delivery sends a fresh export to email recipients or Slack on a configured schedule. This is useful for weekly executive summaries or daily operational reports.

docs.title: Feature Flags and Beta Features
docs.content:
## Feature Flags and Beta Features

Some features are released gradually behind feature flags before becoming available to all users. Beta features are accessible before general availability and may have rough edges.

Opt into beta features from Settings > Preferences > Beta Features. Each feature is listed with a description and the expected general availability date. Enabling a beta feature is reversible.

Organization admins can enable or disable beta features for the entire organization from Settings > Workspace > Beta Features. The admin setting applies to all members.

Beta features are not covered by the standard SLA. If a beta feature causes disruption, disabling it restores the previous behavior.

Provide feedback on beta features using the feedback button that appears when a beta feature is active. Beta feedback is reviewed by the product team and influences the final implementation.

docs.title: Database Connections
docs.content:
## Database Connections

Direct database connections allow you to query your own databases and display results alongside workspace data. Supported databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Microsoft SQL Server, and BigQuery.

Set up a connection from Settings > Integrations > Databases > New Connection. Enter the host, port, database name, and credentials. Use a read-only database user with access only to the schemas needed.

If your database is behind a firewall, allowlist our database connector IP ranges. Alternatively, use our IP tunnel feature (Enterprise only) to connect via an encrypted SSH tunnel.

Once connected, create database views in Analytics > Data Sources. Write a SQL query, name the view, and it becomes available as a data source for reports. SQL queries run with a row limit of 100,000 during preview.

Database connections are tested at creation and re-checked every 15 minutes. A failed health check triggers an alert to workspace admins.

docs.title: Team-Level Permissions
docs.content:
## Team-Level Permissions

Teams allow you to group workspace members and assign permissions at the team level rather than individually. Available on Business and Enterprise plans.

Create a team from Settings > Team > Teams > New Team. Add members and assign team roles (team admin or team member). Team admins can manage team membership but do not gain elevated organization-level permissions.

Assign resources to teams with read or write access. A user's effective access is the combination of their organization role and any team-based access. Team access can expand but not restrict what an organization role already grants.

SCIM group sync maps identity provider groups to workspace teams. When a user is added to or removed from an IdP group, they are automatically added to or removed from the corresponding team.

docs.title: Webhooks vs Polling
docs.content:
## Webhooks vs Polling: When to Use Which

Both approaches retrieve data from the platform but suit different use cases.

Polling: your system calls the API at regular intervals to check for changes. Simple to implement, works behind firewalls that block inbound connections, and does not require a public endpoint. Downsides: latency is limited by your poll interval, and every poll consumes rate limit quota even when nothing has changed.

Webhooks: the platform calls your endpoint when an event occurs. Near-real-time delivery, typically under one second. No wasted rate limit calls when there is no activity. Requires a publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint.

Use polling when: you need data infrequently, your system is behind a firewall, or the latency of your poll interval is acceptable.

Use webhooks when: you need low-latency notifications, high event volume would exhaust poll-based rate limits, or you are building real-time workflows.

docs.title: GraphQL API
docs.content:
## GraphQL API

A GraphQL API is available alongside the REST API. The endpoint is at /api/graphql and accepts POST requests with an application/json body containing a query field.

Authentication uses the same API key as the REST API via the Authorization header.

The GraphQL schema is introspectable. Use the developer console at the developer portal for an interactive explorer with autocomplete and inline documentation.

GraphQL advantages: request exactly the fields you need (no over-fetching), batch multiple queries in one request, and use subscriptions for real-time updates.

Rate limiting for GraphQL is based on query complexity rather than request count. Each field has a cost assigned, and the total cost per request cannot exceed your plan's query budget. Complex queries may hit complexity limits even if request count is low.

docs.title: Data Masking
docs.content:
## Data Masking and Sensitive Fields

Fields containing sensitive information can be marked as sensitive from the field settings. Sensitive fields have additional access controls and display protections.

Masking behavior: sensitive fields show only the last four characters to users without the View Sensitive Data permission. Admins and users explicitly granted view access see the full value. Masking applies in the UI, API responses, and exports.

The View Sensitive Data permission is available on Business and Enterprise plans. Add it to a custom role or grant it to specific users from Settings > Team > Permissions.

Audit logging: every access to a sensitive field (read, write, export) is logged in the audit trail with the user, timestamp, and IP address.

Sensitive fields cannot be used as search or filter criteria by users without view permission.

docs.title: API Client Libraries
docs.content:
## API Client Libraries

Official client libraries are maintained for Python, Node.js (TypeScript), Ruby, Go, and Java.

Installation:
- Python: pip install example-sdk
- Node.js: npm install @example/sdk
- Ruby: gem install example-sdk
- Go: go get github.com/example/sdk-go

All libraries implement automatic retry with exponential backoff on 429 and 5xx responses, cursor-based pagination helpers, request signing, and type-safe response objects.

Libraries are updated within one week of any API change. Pin to a specific major version in production to avoid breaking changes from major version bumps.

For languages without an official library, use the OpenAPI specification available at the developer portal to generate a client using tools like OpenAPI Generator.

docs.title: Structured Logging for API Integrations
docs.content:
## Structured Logging for API Integrations

For production API integrations, structured logging is essential for debugging and observability. Every API response includes headers that should be captured in your logs.

Key headers to log:
- X-Request-ID: unique identifier for the request. Required when contacting support about a specific failure.
- X-RateLimit-Remaining: requests remaining in the current window.
- X-RateLimit-Reset: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets.
- Retry-After: seconds to wait before retrying (only on 429 responses).

Log at minimum: timestamp, request method and URL, response status code, X-Request-ID, and elapsed time.

Correlate your internal trace IDs with X-Request-ID by including your trace ID in the X-Correlation-ID request header. This flows through to our server logs, enabling end-to-end trace correlation.

docs.title: Handling Concurrent Edits via API
docs.content:
## Handling Concurrent Edits via API

When multiple systems or users update the same record concurrently, conflicts can occur. The platform uses optimistic concurrency control to detect and surface these conflicts.

Every record response includes an etag header. When updating a record, include the If-Match header with the etag value. If the record has been modified since you last fetched it, the update fails with HTTP 412 Precondition Failed.

On a 412: re-fetch the record to get the current state and etag, merge your intended changes with the current data, then retry the update with the new etag.

For integrations where conflicts are frequent, use the patch endpoint instead of the full update endpoint. PATCH applies field-level changes rather than replacing the full record, reducing the conflict surface area.

If you do not include an If-Match header, updates succeed regardless of concurrent changes (last write wins).

docs.title: Troubleshooting Integration Errors
docs.content:
## Troubleshooting Integration Errors

When an integration stops working, follow this diagnostic sequence before contacting support.

1. Check the integration status in Settings > Integrations. A red indicator means the connection has failed.

2. Open the integration error log. Each error entry includes a timestamp, the operation attempted, the HTTP status received, and the error response body.

3. Common causes by error type:
   - 401/403: OAuth token expired or revoked. Reconnect via OAuth flow.
   - 404: Resource deleted in the third-party system.
   - 422: Field mapping mismatch. The value format does not match what the third-party expects.
   - 429: Rate limit hit on the third-party side. Reduce sync frequency.
   - 5xx: Third-party system experiencing issues. Check their status page.

4. If the error log is clear but sync is not happening, check the sync schedule and confirm the integration is not paused.

5. Trigger a manual sync to observe behavior in real time.

If none of these steps identify the issue, contact support with the integration name, the error log entries, and the time the issue started.

tickets.subject: Password reset email never arrived
tickets.body: I requested a password reset 20 minutes ago and nothing has shown up in my inbox. I have checked spam and everything.
tickets.resolution: We confirmed the email was sent to the address on file. The customer's corporate email server was rejecting automated messages due to a strict DMARC policy. We asked IT to allowlist our sending domain (mail.supportiq.io). The customer received the email within minutes after the allowlist was applied.

tickets.subject: Reset link says it has expired
tickets.body: I got the reset link in my email but when I click it, it says the link has expired. I'm clicking it right now.
tickets.resolution: The customer had requested three resets in quick succession. Only the most recently issued link is valid, and it had been overwritten by a subsequent request. We asked the customer to request one final reset and use that link without requesting any additional ones. The process completed successfully.

tickets.subject: Account locked after forgotten password attempts
tickets.body: My account got locked because I tried my password too many times. I also need to reset my password but the reset isn't working.
tickets.resolution: We cleared the account lock from the admin panel. We then walked the customer through the correct sequence: first use the lock-cleared account to successfully request a password reset email, then follow the link. We advised them to attempt password reset only after the lock was confirmed cleared, which resolved the issue.

tickets.subject: Admin reset my password but it's not working on login
tickets.body: My admin said they reset my password for me but when I try the link they sent I get an error saying my password is invalid.
tickets.resolution: Admin-initiated password resets generate a one-time link that forces the user to set a new password on first use. The customer had misread the flow and was entering the temporary link as a password. We clarified that clicking the link opens a form to set a new password, and the customer completed the process successfully.

tickets.subject: Lost my phone and can't get past 2FA
tickets.body: I got a new phone and didn't transfer my authenticator app. Now I can't log in because it keeps asking for the 2FA code I don't have.
tickets.resolution: We asked the customer for their backup codes. They did not have them saved. We escalated to their organization admin to verify identity through an internal process. Once verified, the admin contacted us to confirm the identity, and we disabled 2FA on the account. The customer was advised to immediately re-enable 2FA on their new device and securely store backup codes this time.

tickets.subject: 2FA codes are being rejected even when they look correct
tickets.body: I'm entering the 6-digit code from my authenticator app and it says it's wrong. I double-checked the code is current and hasn't expired.
tickets.resolution: The issue was clock drift on the customer's phone. The TOTP algorithm requires the device clock to be within 30 seconds of the server clock. The customer's phone was 2 minutes slow. We asked them to enable automatic time sync in the phone's date/time settings. After the phone time corrected, the codes were accepted.

tickets.subject: How do I move my 2FA to a new authenticator app?
tickets.body: I want to switch from Google Authenticator to Authy. How do I do that without losing access to my account?
tickets.resolution: We explained that you cannot simply re-scan the original QR code because it is no longer shown after initial setup. The correct process is to go to Account Settings > Security, disable 2FA (which requires confirming with a current code), and then re-enable it. During re-enable, a new QR code is displayed that can be scanned into Authy. We advised the customer to complete this while they still have access to their old app.

tickets.subject: Organization enforces 2FA but I can't disable it
tickets.body: I want to turn off 2FA but I don't see the option in my settings. It looks like it might be greyed out.
tickets.resolution: The customer's organization admin had enforced 2FA at the organization level, which prevents individual users from disabling it. We confirmed this was the case and explained that only the organization admin can lift or change this policy. We provided the customer with the admin's contact details from the organization settings and advised them to discuss the policy with their admin.

tickets.subject: API key is being rejected with 401 error
tickets.body: I just generated a new API key and put it in my request header but I'm getting a 401 Unauthorized response. The key looks correct.
tickets.resolution: The customer was including the API key directly in the Authorization header without the 'Bearer' prefix. The correct format is `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`. After updating the header format in their code, the request succeeded. We also suggested they use the API reference in the developer docs which includes a code example for each language.

tickets.subject: Lost my API key — only last 4 digits visible
tickets.body: I forgot to copy my API key after generating it. Now I can only see the last 4 characters. Can you show me the full key?
tickets.resolution: For security reasons, we cannot retrieve or display full API keys after they have been created — only the last four characters are ever stored. We guided the customer to revoke the existing key from Developer Settings and generate a new one. We emphasized the importance of copying and securely storing the key immediately after generation, as this is the only opportunity to view it in full.

tickets.subject: How do I rotate an API key without causing downtime?
tickets.body: We need to rotate our API key for security reasons but our integration is running 24/7 and we can't have any downtime.
tickets.resolution: We walked the customer through the zero-downtime rotation process: generate a new key in Developer Settings (the old key remains active), update the integration to use the new key in a deployment, verify the new key is working in production, then revoke the old key. Both keys can be active simultaneously for as long as needed during the transition. This approach avoids any downtime because the old key only gets revoked after the new one is confirmed working.

tickets.subject: OAuth token expired after 1 hour — do I need to re-authenticate?
tickets.body: Our OAuth access token expired and now our integration is broken. Do users need to log in again every hour?
tickets.resolution: Access tokens expire after one hour by design, but users do not need to re-authenticate. The OAuth flow also issues a refresh token valid for 90 days. The integration should detect a 401 response, use the stored refresh token to request a new access token from the token endpoint, and retry the original request with the new token. We shared a code snippet showing the refresh flow for their SDK. If the refresh token itself expires (90 days of inactivity), a one-time re-authentication is required.

tickets.subject: Getting 429 errors under normal load
tickets.body: We're hitting rate limit errors but our request volume should be well within the Growth plan limits. Something seems wrong.
tickets.resolution: On investigation, the customer's service had multiple instances running, each making requests with the same API key. The rate limit is applied per key, so the combined traffic from all instances was exceeding the per-minute limit. We recommended either distributing traffic across multiple keys (each scoped appropriately) or implementing a centralized rate-limiting layer in their architecture. We also pointed them to the `X-RateLimit-Remaining` header they can monitor to track usage in real time.

tickets.subject: What is the right backoff strategy for 429 errors?
tickets.body: Our integration sometimes hits rate limits and just crashes. We want to implement proper retry logic.
tickets.resolution: We recommended exponential backoff with jitter: wait base * 2^attempt seconds (capped at 60 seconds), plus a small random jitter (0-0.5 seconds) to prevent synchronized retries across multiple instances. Always read the `Retry-After` response header first — if it's present, use that value instead of computing your own. After five consecutive failures, surface the error to your application layer rather than retrying indefinitely. We also pointed the customer to the bulk endpoints which have more favorable rate limits for high-volume scenarios.

tickets.subject: 429 errors even after waiting the Retry-After time
tickets.body: I'm respecting the Retry-After header but I'm still getting 429s immediately when I retry. Why?
tickets.resolution: The customer was applying the Retry-After delay only to the failed request and resuming full traffic volume on all other requests simultaneously. The rate limit window had not fully reset because other concurrent requests were still consuming quota. We explained that Retry-After applies to the entire API key's traffic, not just the single failed request. The integration needed to pause all outgoing requests for the Retry-After duration before resuming. After implementing this correctly, the 429s stopped.

tickets.subject: 500 errors with no useful information in the response body
tickets.body: We keep getting intermittent 500 errors from the API. The response body just says 'internal error' and there's no other info.
tickets.resolution: We asked the customer to capture and share the `request_id` header from the failing responses. With that ID, we located the server-side logs and identified the root cause: a downstream dependency was timing out under heavy load during a specific time window. We applied a fix on the backend and confirmed the errors stopped. We also reminded the customer to always log the `request_id` header in their own error logs, as it dramatically speeds up diagnosis.

tickets.subject: Getting 403 even though my API key should have write access
tickets.body: I can read data fine but when I try to create a record I get a 403 Forbidden. My key should have write access.
tickets.resolution: The customer had generated their API key with only the `read:data` scope selected. Write operations require the `write:data` scope. Since scopes cannot be added to an existing key, we guided the customer to generate a new key with both `read:data` and `write:data` scopes selected. After updating their integration to use the new key, write operations succeeded.

tickets.subject: What does error code 'unprocessable' mean?
tickets.body: I'm getting an HTTP 422 with error code 'unprocessable' but the message doesn't say what's wrong.
tickets.resolution: The 422 unprocessable error means the request is syntactically valid JSON but semantically invalid — a field has an unexpected value or a business rule is violated. We directed the customer to check the `details` field in the error response body, which contains field-level error messages. In their case, the `start_date` they were sending was after the `end_date`, which the API rejects. After correcting the field values, the request succeeded.

tickets.subject: Where can I find my invoices?
tickets.body: I need to download invoices for our accounting department. I can't find where they are in the dashboard.
tickets.resolution: Invoices are located in Settings > Billing > Invoices. Only users with the Admin or Billing Manager role can access this section. We confirmed the customer had the correct role, then walked them through the navigation. Once there, they could see all historical invoices and download PDFs. We also mentioned that invoices are generated within 24 hours of a successful payment, so very recent payments may not yet have an invoice.

tickets.subject: Invoice shows wrong company name and address
tickets.body: Our company recently changed its legal name and our invoices still show the old name. We need this corrected.
tickets.resolution: We directed the customer to update the legal name and billing address in Settings > Billing > Details. We explained that this change applies to future invoices only — past invoices are finalized and cannot be retroactively amended. For their accounting records on past invoices, we suggested issuing a supporting memo from their finance team, as this is standard practice for name-change situations. The updated details appeared correctly on their next monthly invoice.

tickets.subject: VAT not being applied even though we're based in Germany
tickets.body: We're a German company and our invoices don't show any VAT. Is this correct?
tickets.resolution: We checked the customer's account and found they had entered a valid VAT registration number in Settings > Billing > Tax Information. Because they provided a valid VAT ID, our system correctly applies the reverse-charge mechanism and does not charge VAT — the customer is responsible for reporting it through their own VAT return. We explained this is correct EU B2B practice. We also sent a written explanation they could provide to their accountant.

tickets.subject: Invoice for last month hasn't arrived yet
tickets.body: Our monthly invoice usually arrives on the 5th but it's now the 7th and we haven't received it.
tickets.resolution: The customer's payment method had failed on the billing date, which put the invoice in 'open' status and delayed the PDF email notification. We found a charge failure for an expired credit card. We asked the customer to update their payment method in Settings > Billing > Payment Methods and then manually retry the charge from the Invoices page. After the retry succeeded, the invoice PDF was generated and emailed within 24 hours.

tickets.subject: Can I get a refund after cancelling my annual plan?
tickets.body: We cancelled our annual subscription last week, about 3 months into the year. We expected a prorated refund but haven't heard anything.
tickets.resolution: Annual plan cancellations within the first 60 days are eligible for a prorated refund minus a 10% early-termination fee. Since this customer was at the 3-month mark (approximately 90 days), they were past the 60-day window and the annual plan was non-refundable. We explained the policy clearly and provided the exact text from the Terms of Service. We offered to ensure access continued through the full paid annual period. The customer was disappointed but accepted the explanation.

tickets.subject: Charged twice this month
tickets.body: I see two charges on my credit card statement from you this month. I only have one subscription.
tickets.resolution: We reviewed the billing history and identified that the customer had upgraded from the Growth to Business plan mid-cycle. The proration generated an immediate charge for the Business plan's incremental cost for the remaining days of the cycle. Combined with the regular monthly charge already collected, this appeared as two charges. We explained the proration mechanic in detail and confirmed neither charge was erroneous. We also suggested they review the billing estimate preview shown before any plan change, which would have previewed this.

tickets.subject: How do I request a refund for my monthly plan?
tickets.body: I signed up 3 days ago and realized this product isn't the right fit for my team. I'd like a refund.
tickets.resolution: The customer was within the 7-day refund window for new monthly plan subscribers and was fully eligible for a refund. We processed the refund immediately from the billing portal and confirmed it would appear on their credit card statement within 5-10 business days. We cancelled the subscription at the same time so no future charges would occur. We also asked for feedback on why the product wasn't the right fit, which the customer kindly provided.

tickets.subject: How do I export all my data as CSV?
tickets.body: We're doing an audit and need all of our data exported in a format we can open in Excel.
tickets.resolution: We directed the customer to Settings > Data > Export and walked them through selecting CSV format and the date range for their audit. We noted that nested objects in the data would be serialized as JSON strings within CSV cells — for most audit use cases this is fine, but we mentioned the JSON export option if they needed the full nested structure. The export job completed within a few minutes and a download link was sent to their email.

tickets.subject: Export download link expired before I could download it
tickets.body: I received the export email but I didn't download it right away and now the link says it's expired.
tickets.resolution: Export download links expire after 7 days as a security measure — they should not remain accessible indefinitely in email. We initiated a new export job for the same parameters, which completed within 5 minutes. We sent the new download link and asked the customer to download it promptly. For large exports that they need to retain long-term, we recommended setting up scheduled exports to an S3 bucket, where the files persist under their own retention policy.

tickets.subject: Export job stuck in 'processing' for 3 hours
tickets.body: I started a data export this morning and it still says 'processing'. The status hasn't changed in 3 hours.
tickets.resolution: We checked the export job status on the backend. The job had actually completed successfully but a bug in the status webhook caused the UI to remain stuck on 'processing'. The download link had been generated and was accessible via the Export History page even though the status badge showed incorrectly. We directed the customer to Export History to retrieve their link and filed an internal bug for the status display issue. A fix was deployed the same day.

tickets.subject: Can I schedule weekly exports to our S3 bucket?
tickets.body: We want to automatically receive a data export every week without having to manually trigger it.
tickets.resolution: Scheduled exports are supported. We walked the customer through Settings > Data > Export > Schedule, where they could select weekly frequency, choose the format (JSON or CSV), and enter their S3 bucket details including region and IAM credentials. We advised them to use an IAM user with only s3:PutObject permission on the specific bucket path — no broader permissions are needed. After they configured it, we triggered a test write from the UI to confirm the credentials worked before the first scheduled run.

tickets.subject: Large export split into multiple files — which order do I combine them?
tickets.body: My export came as 12 separate files. How do I know the order they go in and how to combine them?
tickets.resolution: For exports split into multiple files, each filename includes a sequential part number: `export_2024-01-01_part001.json`, `export_2024-01-01_part002.json`, and so on. They are already sorted and numbered in the correct order. To recombine JSON exports, concatenate the arrays from each file. For CSV, concatenate the files with the header row from the first file only. We shared a short Python snippet for combining them. We also noted that Parquet files can be read as a directory by tools like DuckDB without needing to manually combine them.

tickets.subject: Need to give a contractor view-only access
tickets.body: We have an external auditor who needs to see our data but shouldn't be able to change anything. What role do I assign?
tickets.resolution: The Viewer role is exactly what the customer needed. Viewers have read-only access to all data and reports but cannot create, edit, or delete anything. We walked the customer through Settings > Team > Invite Members, entering the auditor's email and selecting the Viewer role. The invitation email was sent, and after the auditor accepted, we confirmed from the Team page that their role showed as Viewer. We also noted that the auditor will be counted as a seat only if they are on a paid plan.

tickets.subject: Admin accidentally removed the only other admin
tickets.body: I removed my co-admin from the team by mistake. Now I'm the only admin left. Is there anything I can do?
tickets.resolution: Since the customer was still an admin themselves, they could re-invite the removed user from Settings > Team > Invite Members and assign them the Admin role again. The invited user would need to accept the invitation. We confirmed there is no 'undo' for removal — re-inviting is the correct path. We also pointed out that the platform prevents an admin from removing themselves if they are the last admin, as a safeguard, but this does not apply to removing other admins.

tickets.subject: Member can't see the billing section
tickets.body: One of our team members needs to view invoices but they say they can't see the billing section at all.
tickets.resolution: The Billing section in Settings is restricted to Admin and Billing Manager roles. The customer's team member had the Member role, which does not include billing access by design. The customer had two options: upgrade the team member to Admin (which grants full access, not just billing) or, if on the Business plan, create a custom Billing Manager role with only billing permissions. We described both options and the customer chose to create a custom role.

tickets.subject: How does role change affect a user who is currently logged in?
tickets.body: If I change someone's role from Admin to Member right now while they're actively using the app, what happens?
tickets.resolution: Role changes take effect immediately on the server side. The affected user will see their permissions change on their next page load — they do not need to log out and back in. If they try to access an Admin-only feature before their page refreshes, they will receive a 403 Forbidden error, which will prompt a page reload that picks up the new role. We advised the customer that it's courteous to notify the user before downgrading their role, but technically no coordination is required.

tickets.subject: Invite expired before new member could accept
tickets.body: I invited a new teammate a week and a half ago and they just told me they never saw the email. The invite link they got is expired.
tickets.resolution: Team invitations expire after 7 days. We guided the customer to Settings > Team > Pending Invitations, where the expired invite was still listed. They could resend the invite directly from that view, which generates a fresh 7-day link. We also suggested the customer first check that the email address in the pending invite was spelled correctly — in this case it was correct, and the email had gone to the recipient's spam folder. After resending and the recipient checking spam, they accepted successfully.

tickets.subject: Integration setup — what's the checklist for a new account?
tickets.body: We just signed up and I want to make sure we don't miss any important setup steps. Is there an onboarding guide?
tickets.resolution: We directed the customer to the Onboarding Checklist in the Help Center, which covers all 10 steps from email verification through team invites. We also walked through the most commonly missed steps: enabling 2FA, configuring notification preferences, and setting up SSO before inviting large numbers of users (to avoid mixed authentication states). We offered to schedule a 30-minute onboarding call, which the customer accepted.

tickets.subject: Invited teammates but they say they got no email
tickets.body: I sent invites to 5 teammates and none of them received the invitation email.
tickets.resolution: We checked the delivery logs and confirmed all five invitation emails were sent successfully from our side. The issue was that the customer's organization used a strict email allowlist managed by their IT department. All external emails were blocked by default. We provided the customer with our sending domain (mail.supportiq.io) and asked them to have IT add it to the allowlist. After the allowlist was updated, we resent all five invites and they arrived within minutes.

tickets.subject: Can't complete onboarding — email not verified
tickets.body: Some features are greyed out and I keep seeing a banner about verifying my email. I clicked the verify link but it didn't work.
tickets.resolution: The customer's verification link had expired (they had clicked it 3 days after receiving it). We resent a new verification email from the Admin panel. The customer checked their inbox, clicked the fresh link immediately, and the features became available right away. We also noted that the verification email can be resent by the user from Account Settings > Profile if they ever need to trigger it themselves.

tickets.subject: Confused about where to start with first integration
tickets.body: I'm new to the platform and I want to connect our database. I have the credentials but I don't know where to go.
tickets.resolution: We directed the customer to the Integrations page from the main navigation sidebar. Database connections are under the 'Data Sources' category. The customer needed the host, port, username, password, and database name. We walked through each field in the connection form. After entering the credentials, we used the 'Test Connection' button, which confirmed the connection worked. We then explained how to run a first query to verify data was flowing correctly.

tickets.subject: Zapier integration not showing our account events
tickets.body: I set up the Zapier integration but when I try to create a Zap I don't see any of the trigger events we need.
tickets.resolution: The Zapier integration uses polling-based triggers, which means it checks for new events at intervals rather than receiving them in real time. The events the customer was looking for were available but only appear after the first poll cycle runs (approximately every 15 minutes). We also confirmed that the trigger events needed were part of the Growth plan, which the customer was on. After waiting one polling cycle, the triggers appeared as expected in the Zapier UI.

tickets.subject: Slack notifications stopped after we changed Slack workspace
tickets.body: We moved to a new Slack workspace and now we're not getting any notifications. The integration appears connected.
tickets.resolution: The integration was connected to the old workspace. Even though the UI showed a 'connected' status, the OAuth token was specific to the original workspace and was invalid for the new one. We guided the customer to Settings > Integrations > Slack, clicked 'Disconnect', and then reconnected by clicking 'Connect' which triggered the Slack OAuth flow for the new workspace. After reconnecting, we reconfigured the channel mappings and notifications resumed.

tickets.subject: Salesforce sync keeps failing for some records
tickets.body: Our Salesforce integration syncs most records fine but about 5% fail every run. The error log just says 'field mapping error'.
tickets.resolution: We reviewed the integration error log entries with the customer and identified that the failing records had a custom field in Salesforce that was not mapped in the integration settings. Field mapping is configured in Settings > Integrations > Salesforce > Field Mapping. The custom field needed to be added to the mapping manually. After adding it and running a manual sync, the failure rate dropped to zero. We also showed the customer how to set up failure alert notifications so they are proactively informed of sync errors.

tickets.subject: Integration shows 'disconnected' status with no explanation
tickets.body: Our third-party integration started showing 'disconnected' with no warning. We haven't changed anything.
tickets.resolution: The disconnected status is typically caused by an OAuth token being revoked or expiring on the third-party side. In this case, the customer's Salesforce admin had rotated the OAuth credentials for the integration user in Salesforce, which invalidated the token stored in our system. We guided the customer to click 'Reconnect' in the integration settings, which initiated a fresh OAuth flow with the updated Salesforce credentials. After reconnecting, sync resumed automatically at the next scheduled interval.

tickets.subject: Webhook isn't receiving events — all deliveries failing
tickets.body: I set up a webhook endpoint but none of the events are coming through. The delivery log shows failures.
tickets.resolution: We reviewed the delivery log and the failures showed HTTP 403 responses from the customer's endpoint. Their server had a firewall rule that blocked requests from our IP ranges. We provided the customer with our static egress IP list (documented in the developer docs under Webhook Security) and asked them to allowlist those IPs. After the firewall was updated, we manually triggered a test ping from the webhook settings, which succeeded, and subsequent events delivered normally.

tickets.subject: How do I verify the webhook signature in Python?
tickets.body: I'm receiving webhook events but I want to verify the signature to make sure they're coming from you. How do I do that in Python?
tickets.resolution: We provided the customer with a Python code snippet: use `hmac.new(secret.encode(), request_body, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()` and compare it to the `X-Signature-SHA256` header value. The key point we emphasized is to use `hmac.compare_digest()` for the comparison rather than `==` to prevent timing attacks. We also reminded them that the signature is computed over the raw request body bytes, not a parsed JSON object, so they should read the body before parsing it.

tickets.subject: Webhook was auto-disabled — how do I re-enable it?
tickets.body: I got an email saying my webhook was disabled. I fixed the endpoint issue. How do I turn it back on?
tickets.resolution: Webhooks that fail consistently for 48 hours are automatically disabled to reduce unnecessary traffic to unavailable endpoints. To re-enable it, go to Settings > Integrations > Webhooks, find the disabled webhook (it will show a 'Disabled' badge), and click 'Enable'. We also recommended first using the 'Send Test Ping' button to confirm the endpoint is responding correctly before re-enabling, so you do not restart the automatic-disable clock immediately.

tickets.subject: SSO login fails with 'audience mismatch' error
tickets.body: We set up SSO but when users try to log in through Okta they get an 'audience mismatch' error.
tickets.resolution: The 'audience mismatch' error in SAML means the Entity ID in the SAML assertion from Okta did not match the Entity ID we expected. The customer had manually typed our Entity ID in Okta rather than copying it from our SSO settings page. There was a single extra slash at the end of the URL they had entered. We asked them to copy the Entity ID directly from Settings > Security > SSO and update it in Okta. After saving and retesting, SSO login worked correctly.

tickets.subject: New SSO users created with wrong role
tickets.body: Users who log in via SSO are all being created as Members, but we want them to be Viewers by default.
tickets.resolution: When no `role` attribute is included in the SAML assertion, new users default to the Member role. To change this, the customer had two options: configure the `role` attribute in the SAML assertion from their IdP to explicitly set the role, or change the default SSO role from Settings > Security > SSO > Default User Role. The customer chose to set the default to Viewer in the SSO settings, which required no changes on the IdP side. New SSO logins after that were created as Viewers.

tickets.subject: SSO setup test fails with 'clock skew' error
tickets.body: When I test the SSO configuration it immediately fails with a message about clock skew. Our IdP is Okta.
tickets.resolution: SAML assertions include timestamps with a short validity window (typically 5 minutes). If the server clock on either side is out of sync by more than that window, assertion validation fails. The customer's Okta instance was running on a server whose clock had drifted 7 minutes. We recommended checking the Okta system status and contacting Okta support to correct the clock sync. As a temporary workaround, we extended our tolerance window to 10 minutes from Settings > Security > SSO > Advanced. Once Okta corrected the clock, the tolerance was returned to normal.

tickets.subject: SSO users can't set a local password
tickets.body: Some of our team members that we invited through SSO are asking how to set a password so they can log in without SSO if needed.
tickets.resolution: This is expected behavior for SSO-provisioned accounts. When an organization has SSO configured and enforced, SSO-provisioned users are not allowed to set a local password — authentication must go through the IdP. This is a security feature to ensure all access goes through the centralized identity provider where access revocation is managed. If the customer needs fallback access for emergency situations (e.g., IdP outage), they should consult their IdP vendor's availability documentation rather than creating local password fallbacks.

tickets.subject: API responses are extremely slow — 10+ second latency
tickets.body: Our integration has been seeing API response times of 10-15 seconds for the past two days. It was much faster before.
tickets.resolution: We reviewed the performance metrics for the customer's queries and identified that they were making unbounded queries against a table that had grown to 50 million rows over the past month. The lack of a date filter or row limit meant each query was scanning the entire table. We recommended adding a `created_after` filter to limit the query to a relevant time window and enabling server-side pagination using the `limit` and `cursor` parameters. After implementing these changes, response times dropped to under 300ms.

tickets.subject: Getting 504 timeout errors on large data operations
tickets.body: When I run our nightly data processing job via the API I keep getting 504 gateway timeouts. It worked fine a few months ago.
tickets.resolution: API requests that take longer than 30 seconds are terminated with a 504. The customer's dataset had grown over time, making the synchronous operation too slow. We recommended migrating to the async bulk processing endpoint: submit the job, receive a job ID, and poll `/jobs/{id}/status` until complete. We provided a code example in Python for the async pattern. The job now runs asynchronously with no timeout risk, and the customer receives the result when it is ready rather than blocking.

tickets.subject: How do I enable query profiling to see what's slow?
tickets.body: Our requests are slower than expected and I want to understand where the time is being spent.
tickets.resolution: We guided the customer to Settings > Developer > Profiling and enabled query profiling for their account. Once enabled, every API response includes extra headers: `X-Timing-DB` (milliseconds in the database), `X-Timing-Serialize` (serialization time), and `X-Timing-Network` (estimated network overhead). We showed the customer how to compare these headers between a slow request and a fast one to isolate the bottleneck. Their specific issue was high `X-Timing-Serialize` time, indicating too many fields being requested — they switched to field selection using the `fields` query parameter, which resolved it.

tickets.subject: High CPU on our server correlates with API polling loop
tickets.body: We're polling the API every 2 seconds to check for new events and our server's CPU usage has spiked. Is there a better way?
tickets.resolution: Polling at 2-second intervals generates significant load on both the customer's server and our API, and it is rarely necessary with proper webhook support. We recommended replacing the polling loop with webhooks: configure a webhook subscription for the event types they care about, and our system will push events to their endpoint within seconds of them occurring. This eliminates the polling loop entirely. We also noted that high-frequency polling can trigger rate limiting on their API key. After switching to webhooks, their CPU usage dropped substantially.

tickets.subject: N+1 query pattern is causing thousands of API calls
tickets.body: Our integration makes one API call to get a list of records and then one more call per record to get details. This adds up to thousands of calls.
tickets.resolution: This is a classic N+1 pattern. We pointed the customer to our bulk fetch endpoint: instead of calling `/records/{id}` for each item, they can call `/records?ids=id1,id2,id3,...` with up to 100 IDs per call. This reduces thousands of API calls to dozens. We shared the API reference section on bulk operations and a code example showing how to batch the IDs from the initial list response. After implementing this, the customer's total API calls per sync cycle dropped from ~4,000 to ~40.

tickets.subject: Indexes missing after data migration
tickets.body: We migrated our data to a new workspace and now queries that were fast before are extremely slow.
tickets.resolution: When data is migrated to a new workspace, indexes from the source workspace are not automatically carried over. The customer needed to recreate them in Settings > Data > Indexes in the new workspace. We helped them identify which fields had been indexed in the old workspace by reviewing the query profiling results from before the migration (which they had kept in their logs). After recreating the indexes — primarily on `created_at`, `status`, and a custom ID field — query performance returned to the previous baseline.

tickets.subject: What should I do first when setting up a new account?
tickets.body: We just created our company account. There are a lot of settings and I don't know what's important to do first.
tickets.resolution: We walked the customer through the priority order: (1) verify the organization admin email, (2) set up SSO if the company uses an identity provider — this must be done before inviting many users, (3) configure 2FA policy for the organization, (4) invite team members with appropriate roles, (5) connect the first data source or integration, and (6) configure notification preferences. We also shared the link to the Onboarding Checklist in the Help Center which documents all ten steps in detail.

tickets.subject: Can't find where to enable SSO — is it on my plan?
tickets.body: I want to set up SSO for our team but I can't find it in the security settings.
tickets.resolution: SSO/SAML is available on the Business and Enterprise plans. The customer was on the Growth plan, which does not include SSO. The SSO option in Settings > Security is hidden entirely for plans that do not support it. We confirmed the plan level and explained the feature gating. We also provided a comparison of what's included on each plan and mentioned that upgrading to Business would immediately unlock the SSO setup flow. The customer decided to upgrade.

tickets.subject: Salesforce sync frequency — what's the minimum interval?
tickets.body: We need near-real-time sync with Salesforce. What's the fastest sync schedule we can configure?
tickets.resolution: The minimum sync interval for scheduled Salesforce sync is 15 minutes. For use cases requiring faster propagation, we recommended supplementing the scheduled sync with event-driven updates: configure Salesforce Process Builder or Flow to call our webhook endpoint whenever a relevant record changes. This can achieve effective latency of under a minute. We shared the webhook endpoint URL and authentication requirements. The customer implemented the hybrid approach and achieved the near-real-time sync they needed.

tickets.subject: Webhook signature verification keeps failing intermittently
tickets.body: Most webhook events verify fine but occasionally the signature check fails even though the payload looks correct.
tickets.resolution: Intermittent signature verification failures usually indicate a body-parsing issue. The customer's web framework was automatically parsing the request body as JSON before the signature check code ran. Since the signature is computed over the raw bytes of the request body, any modification (including reformatting by a JSON parser) changes the body and breaks the signature. We advised the customer to read the raw request body bytes before any parsing happens, compute the signature on those bytes, and then separately parse the body for processing. After this fix, all verifications passed.
